_meta.source: edhamma/vism (Ñāṇamoli, Visuddhimagga: The Path of Purification)
_meta.license: BPS-copyrighted translation. Edhamma awaits BPS permission. Citation + link is fair use; full-text bundling requires explicit BPS approval.
_meta.note: Glossary entries are short dictionary-style definitions, not full text. Each entry may reference one or more Visuddhimagga sections via vismRefs (Roman.arabic format like "III.68").

aṅga.title: aṅga
aṅga.gloss: (l) limb, (2) factor (of path, jhāna, etc.), (3) practice, etc

attha.title: attha
attha.gloss: (1) benefit, result, (2) purpose, aim, goal, (3) meaning

adinnādāna.title: adinnādāna
adinnādāna.gloss: taking what is not given, stealing

*adhikicca.title: *adhikicca
*adhikicca.gloss: as an integral part of, dependent on

adhisīla.title: adhisīla
adhisīla.gloss: higher virtue (i.e. virtue as basis for jhāna and insight)

anuloma.title: anuloma
anuloma.gloss: (1) in conformity with, (2) in forward order, or as “arising” (of dependent origination), (3) conformity (stage in development of jhāna or insight)

abhibhāyatana.title: abhibhāyatana
abhibhāyatana.gloss: base of mastery, base for transcending (the sense-desire sphere)

arahant.title: arahant
arahant.gloss: arahant (4th and last stage of realization)

ariya.title: ariya
ariya.gloss: noble, noble one (i.e. one who has attained a path)

*allīyituṃ.title: *allīyituṃ
*allīyituṃ.gloss: to give shelter (not in CPD; inf. ā+līyati; see leṇa in CPD): (allīyitabba) ; (allīyana)

avyākata.title: avyākata
avyākata.gloss: (1) (kammically) indeterminate (i.e. neither profitable nor unprofitable), (2) un-answered (by the Buddha)

asekha.title: asekha
asekha.gloss: non-trainer (i.e. one who has reached the fruition of arahantship)

ākāra-rūpa.title: ākāra-rūpa
ākāra-rūpa.gloss: matter as mode (e.g. “mark of the female”)

ārammaṇa.title: ārammaṇa
ārammaṇa.gloss: object (of consciousness or its concomitants), support

iddhipāda.title: iddhipāda
iddhipāda.gloss: road to power, basis for success (the 4)

*uppatti.title: *uppatti
*uppatti.gloss: also origin of a sutta (tech. term)

uppatti-bhava.title: uppatti-bhava
uppatti-bhava.gloss: rebirth-process becoming, being as result of action

*okāseti.title: *okāseti
*okāseti.gloss: to scatter on (not as in PED): ( )

ojaṭṭhamaka.title: ojaṭṭhamaka
ojaṭṭhamaka.gloss: material octad with nutritive essence as eighth

*kataka.title: *kataka
*kataka.gloss: also a kind of seed (used for clearing water)

kathā-vatthu.title: kathā-vatthu
kathā-vatthu.gloss: (1) name of Abhidhamma book, (2) instance of talk (the 10)

kalāpa.title: kalāpa
kalāpa.gloss: (1) group, (2) material group (term for material octad, etc.)

kalāpa-sammasana.title: kalāpa-sammasana
kalāpa-sammasana.gloss: comprehension by groups (does not refer to the material octad, etc.)

kasiṇa.title: kasiṇa
kasiṇa.gloss: kasiṇa, universal (a contemplation device, and concept based thereon)

kāma-guṇa.title: kāma-guṇa
kāma-guṇa.gloss: cord of sense-desire (the 5), dimension of sensual desire

kāya.title: kāya
kāya.gloss: (1) body, group, order, (2) the material body, (3) the mental body (i.e. the 3 nāmakkhandha)

kāya-saṅkhāra.title: kāya-saṅkhāra
kāya-saṅkhāra.gloss: bodily formation (term for in-breath and out-breath)

*koṭṭhaṭṭhi.title: *koṭṭhaṭṭhi
*koṭṭhaṭṭhi.gloss: shoulder-blade bone (lit. “flat-bone”; not as in PED)

*gata-paccāgata.title: *gata-paccāgata
*gata-paccāgata.gloss: (1) duty of going to and returning from the alms round with the meditation subject, (2) kind of refuse rag

catusamuṭṭhāna.title: catusamuṭṭhāna
catusamuṭṭhāna.gloss: (matter) of fourfold origination (i.e. by consciousness, kamma, temperature and nutriment)

*cāpalya.title: *cāpalya
*cāpalya.gloss: also personal vanity: (this meaning not in CPD, under acāpalya or acapala)

citta-saṅkhāra.title: citta-saṅkhāra
citta-saṅkhāra.gloss: mental formation (term for perception and feeling)

cetasika.title: cetasika
cetasika.gloss: consciousness concomitant (i.e. feeling, perception and formations)

ṭhiti.title: ṭhiti
ṭhiti.gloss: (1) presence, (2) station, (3) relation, (4) steadiness, stability, (5) stationariness, stagnation

*tadārammaṇa.title: *tadārammaṇa
*tadārammaṇa.gloss: (1) having that (aforesaid thing) as its object, (2) registration (consciousness)

tilakkhaṇa.title: tilakkhaṇa
tilakkhaṇa.gloss: three characteristics (of impermanence, pain and not-self)

*ti-santati-rūpa.title: *ti-santati-rūpa
*ti-santati-rūpa.gloss: materiality of triple continuity (term for the three decads at moment of rebirth-linking)

*ti-samuṭṭhāna.title: *ti-samuṭṭhāna
*ti-samuṭṭhāna.gloss: materiality of triple origination (by kamma, temperature and nutriment only)

*theriya.title: *theriya
*theriya.gloss: belonging to the Elders: epil. verse

dassana.title: dassana
dassana.gloss: (l) seeing (the eye’s function), (2) vision, (3) term for the first path

*desantar-uppatti.title: *desantar-uppatti
*desantar-uppatti.gloss: successive arising in adjacent locations (description of phenomenon of motion)

dvāra.title: dvāra
dvāra.gloss: door (i.e. the 6 d. of consciousness by the 6 bases; also the 3 d. of kamma by body, speech and mind)

dhamma.title: dhamma
dhamma.gloss: (1) the Dhamma or Law (as discovered by the Buddha), (2) dhamma, state, thing, phenomenon, (3) mental object, mental datum (12th base)

dhamma-ṭṭhiti-ñāṇa.title: dhamma-ṭṭhiti-ñāṇa
dhamma-ṭṭhiti-ñāṇa.gloss: knowledge of relations of states, knowledge of structure of ideas

*dhura-bhatta.title: *dhura-bhatta
*dhura-bhatta.gloss: meal given in a principal house (not as in PED)

nāma-kaya.title: nāma-kaya
nāma-kaya.gloss: mentality body, mental body (aggregates of feeling, perception and formations)

nāma-rūpa.title: nāma-rūpa
nāma-rūpa.gloss: mentality-materiality (term for the five aggregates, or for the four aggregates excluding consciousness)

*nipphanna.title: *nipphanna
*nipphanna.gloss: produced (term for certain kinds of derived materiality)

Nibbāna.title: Nibbāna
Nibbāna.gloss: nirvana, extinction (of greed, hate and delusion)

niyyāna.title: niyyāna
niyyāna.gloss: outlet (from the round of rebirths; term for the path)

nissaya.title: nissaya
nissaya.gloss: (1) support, (2) the dependence (given by teacher to pupil)

pakati.title: pakati
pakati.gloss: (1) nature, natural, normal, (2) Primordial Essence, Prakṛti

pakkhandati.title: pakkhandati
pakkhandati.gloss: to enter into, to launch out into

paccaya.title: paccaya
paccaya.gloss: (1) condition (for what is conditionally arisen), (2) requisite (the 4 for the bhikkhu)

paccayākāra.title: paccayākāra
paccayākāra.gloss: structure of conditions (term for dependent origination)

paññatti.title: paññatti
paññatti.gloss: (1) making-known, announcement, (2) appellation, designation, (3) concept, description

paṭicca.title: paṭicca
paṭicca.gloss: (indecl. ger. of paṭiyeti) having depended, due to, dependent on

*paṭicca.title: *paṭicca
*paṭicca.gloss: (decl. adj.) ought to be arrived at

papañca.title: papañca
papañca.gloss: (1) obstacle, (2) diffuseness, (2) diversification (as function of craving, conceit and wrong view; not in this sense in PED)

*pabhuti.title: *pabhuti
*pabhuti.gloss: missing parenthesis in BPS2011?: encl.) and so on, etcetera ( = ādi in that sense): , ; , etc

pariggaha.title: pariggaha
pariggaha.gloss: (1) inclusion, (2) embracing (as definition of right speech), (3) reinforcement, (4) discerning, etc

paritta.title: paritta
paritta.gloss: (1) small, (2) limited (term for the sense-desire sphere), (3) protection (term for certain discourses recited for that purpose)

*pariveṇa.title: *pariveṇa
*pariveṇa.gloss: also surroundings of a building, surrounding walk

pavatta, pavatti.title: pavatta, pavatti
pavatta, pavatti.gloss: (1) occurrence, (2) course of an existence (between rebirth-linking and death)

*piṇḍika.title: *piṇḍika
*piṇḍika.gloss: the calf of the leg: ; ; cf. piṇḍa at

puthujjana.title: puthujjana
puthujjana.gloss: ordinary man (i.e. one who has not reached the path)

phala.title: phala
phala.gloss: (1) fruit of (plants), (2) fruit of cause, (3) fruition (of path; the 4)

bala.title: bala
bala.gloss: power (the 5; the 10 of a Perfect One)

brahmacariya.title: brahmacariya
brahmacariya.gloss: life of purity, the good life, the life divine

*bhanti.title: *bhanti
*bhanti.gloss: they shine (3rd p. pl. of bhāti): ( )

bhāvanā.title: bhāvanā
bhāvanā.gloss: (1) development (lit. making be’), (2) term for the 3 higher paths

bhūta.title: bhūta
bhūta.gloss: (1) become, been, (2) creature, (3) primary element (entity) of matter, etc

bhūtūpādā-rūpa.title: bhūtūpādā-rūpa
bhūtūpādā-rūpa.gloss: matter derived upon the (four) primary elements (the 24 kinds)

mahaggata.title: mahaggata
mahaggata.gloss: exalted (a term for consciousness “exalted” from the “limited” sense-desire sphere to the fine-material or immaterial spheres)

mātikā.title: mātikā
mātikā.gloss: (1) schedule of the Abhidhamma, (2) codes of the Pātimokkha (the 2), (3) schedule, etc

yathā-kammūpaga-ñāṇa.title: yathā-kammūpaga-ñāṇa
yathā-kammūpaga-ñāṇa.gloss: knowledge of (beings’) faring according to deeds

rasa.title: rasa
rasa.gloss: (1) taste, flavour, (2) nature as function or achievement, (3) stimulus (for feeling), (4) essential juice, filtrate

rūpa.title: rūpa
rūpa.gloss: (1) materiality (aggregate), fine materiality of fine-material Brahmāworld, matter in general, material form, (2) visible datum, visible object, visible matter, visible form

rūpa-rūpa.title: rūpa-rūpa
rūpa-rūpa.gloss: concrete materiality (term for certain derived kinds of materiality)

lokiya.title: lokiya
lokiya.gloss: mundane (i.e. not associated with the path, fruition or Nibbāna)

lokuttara.title: lokuttara
lokuttara.gloss: supramundane (i.e. the 9 states consisting of the 4 paths, 4 fruitions and Nibbāna, and states associated with them)

vaṭṭa.title: vaṭṭa
vaṭṭa.gloss: round (of kamma, etc.; term for the dependent origination as arising)

vatthu.title: vatthu
vatthu.gloss: (1) basis, physical basis (term for the six internal bases), (2) object, (3) instance, example, (4) story, etc

vikāra-rūpa.title: vikāra-rūpa
vikāra-rūpa.gloss: materiality as alteration (term for certain of the 24 kinds of derived materiality, i.e. impermanence, etc.)

vikubbana.title: vikubbana
vikubbana.gloss: (1) versatility (in development of divine abidings), (2) transformation (by supernormal power)

vinaya.title: vinaya
vinaya.gloss: (1) Vinaya Piṭaka or Book of Discipline, (2) discipline, removal, leading away

vipassanā.title: vipassanā
vipassanā.gloss: insight (the vision of what is formed as impermanent, painful, notself)

vivaṭṭa.title: vivaṭṭa
vivaṭṭa.gloss: (1) cessation of the round (of kamma, etc.), the dependent origination as cessation, (2) turning away, (3) expansion (of world after contraction)

vihāra.title: vihāra
vihāra.gloss: (1) dwelling place, abode, (2) monastery, (3) mode of abiding

vīthi-citta.title: vīthi-citta
vīthi-citta.gloss: a consciousness of the cognitive series

vuṭṭhānagāmini-vipassanā.title: vuṭṭhānagāmini-vipassanā
vuṭṭhānagāmini-vipassanā.gloss: insight leading to emergence (of the path)

vedanā.title: vedanā
vedanā.gloss: feeling (i.e. of pleasure, pain, or neither)

vodāna.title: vodāna
vodāna.gloss: cleansing (term for consciousness preceding absorption or path)

*saṅkara.title: *saṅkara
*saṅkara.gloss: confounding, confusing: ; epil. verses (see CPD asaṅkara)

*saṅku-patha.title: *saṅku-patha
*saṅku-patha.gloss: also a path set on piles

satta-saññā.title: satta-saññā
satta-saññā.gloss: (1) perception of a living being, (2) the seven perceptions (first of the 18 principal insights)

*sappati.title: *sappati
*sappati.gloss: to be emitted (pass. of sapati, to swear)

samuccheda.title: samuccheda
samuccheda.gloss: cutting off (of defilements by the path)

*sampatta-visaya.title: *sampatta-visaya
*sampatta-visaya.gloss: having a contiguous objective field (i.e. smell, taste and touch)

sāmañña-lakkhaṇa.title: sāmañña-lakkhaṇa
sāmañña-lakkhaṇa.gloss: general characteristic (of what is formed, i.e. the 3 beginning with impermanence)

sīlabbata.title: sīlabbata
sīlabbata.gloss: rules and vows (Ñāṇamoli’s original translation was “rites and rituals,” but was changed in accordance with his later translation of this term. )

sukkha-vipassaka.title: sukkha-vipassaka
sukkha-vipassaka.gloss: bare(or dry-) insight worker (one who attains the path without previously having attained jhāna)

sukha.title: sukha
sukha.gloss: pleasure, pleasant, bliss, blissful, bodily pleasure

sekha.title: sekha
sekha.gloss: trainer (term for one possessing one of the four paths or first three fruitions, so with training still to do)
